full_prompt: Design the HV battery enclosure for a student-built electric off-road recreational vehicle. Per a governing structural-demonstration rulebook, the electric variant carries a high-voltage tractive battery (nominal 72 V, capacity 60 Ah => 4.3 kWh, total pack mass 35 kg) sealed inside a structural enclosure rated IP67, mounted behind the driver inside the cage. Enclosure shall be a welded monolithic aluminum 6082-T6 box, E = 70 GPa, yield = 250 MPa, ultimate = 290 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. Overall bounding box 500 mm (X) x 300 mm (Y) x 250 mm (Z), wall thickness to be sized. Mounting: 6x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts through frame brackets. Load cases (electric-variant subset of standard off-road chassis load factors): (R1) Front impact 4 g applied to the full 35 kg pack: 4 x 35 x 9.81 = 1,374 N +X direction into the cells; max von Mises in enclosure walls <= yield / 1.25 = 200 MPa. (R2) Side impact 3 g: 3 x 35 x 9.81 = 1,030 N +Y; max von Mises <= 200 MPa. (R3) Rollover 2 g vertical + roof crush: 2 x 35 x 9.81 = 687 N vertical; max von Mises <= 200 MPa. (R4) IP67 qualification requires a gasket-sealed lid: the bolted lid interface must maintain compression on an EPDM gasket (nominal 5 mm initial thickness, compression 25%, E_gasket 5 MPa) across all load cases; lid-to-box edge gap <= 0.1 mm under worst-case inertial loading. (R5) Enclosure mass (excluding cells) <= 7 kg. (R6) Rear-impact 3 g: 3 x 35 x 9.81 = 1,030 N -X; max von Mises <= 200 MPa. (R7) First modal frequency >= 40 Hz. (R8) Tractive-system nominal voltage <= 96 V DC (electric-variant rule). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x4 cases) + gasket contact FEA for R4 + modal for R7. Vibration secondary via R7. Thermal, fluid, radiation explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded enclosure (single FEA body), weld map, gasket spec, and a report listing enclosure mass in kg, max von Mises per load case, max lid gap in mm, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design the HV battery enclosure for a student-built electric off-road recreational vehicle compliant with the governing structural-demonstration rulebook for the electric variant. The enclosure is a welded monolithic aluminum 6082-T6 box rated IP67, housing a 72 V / 60 Ah (~4.3 kWh) tractive battery (pack mass 35 kg). Tractive-system nominal voltage <= 96 V DC. Mounting is 6 x M8 Grade 8.8 bolts through frame brackets; lid uses a 5 mm EPDM gasket at 25% compression.

Envelope: outer bounding 500 x 300 x 250 mm (X x Y x Z).

Load cases (inertial loads on the full 35 kg pack).
- LC1 front impact 4 g: 1,374 N in +X.
- LC2 side impact 3 g: 1,030 N in +Y.
- LC3 rollover 2 g vertical: 687 N vertical.
- LC4 rear impact 3 g: 1,030 N in -X.
- LC5 IP67 gasket compression: under worst-case inertial load, lid-to-box edge gap <= 0.1 mm.
- LC6 modal: first frequency >= 40 Hz.

prompt.title: Student off-road EV high-voltage battery enclosure
prompt.brief: 6082-T6 welded battery box for 35 kg HV pack, IP67 sealed, survives 4/3/2/3 g triaxial loads.

boundary_conditions.internal_mass: 35 kg lumped and tied to cell-mount interior

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Pack thermal management is a separate EV gate.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: IP67 qualification is a physical test, gap-based FEA is proxy.
